md: **Integers** are the whole numbers including their negatives: $\ldots, -3, -2, -1, 0, 1, 2, 3, \ldots$. Adding and subtracting them is the foundation of everything that comes next.
### Picture it on a number line

Every integer has a spot on the number line. **Adding** moves you along it: a positive number moves you *right*, a negative moves you *left*. Try it below — change the start point and the amount you add, and watch the arrow.
**Same signs** — add the magnitudes, keep the sign.
**Different signs** — subtract the smaller magnitude from the larger, keep the sign of the larger.
$7 + (-3) = 4$ (larger magnitude is positive, so result is positive).
$-7 + 3 = -4$ (larger magnitude is negative, so result is negative).

blocks.title: Subtraction = adding the opposite
blocks.body: $5 - 3 = 5 + (-3) = 2$. The minus sign just flips the sign of the next number, then you add. This means every subtraction is really an addition — and the rules above cover both.

md: $-8 + 3 = -5$. Different signs — subtract magnitudes ($|8| - |3| = 5$), keep the sign of the bigger magnitude (negative).
$-2 - 7 = -2 + (-7) = -9$. Same signs after rewriting subtraction — add magnitudes ($2 + 7 = 9$), keep the negative sign.
$5 - (-4) = 5 + 4 = 9$. Subtracting a negative is adding a positive.

blocks.title: Recap
blocks.body: Addition is movement on the number line. **Same signs add; different signs subtract; subtraction is just adding the opposite.** Master these and you'll never have to think hard about them again.
